title: Lesson 105: (Grammar) - polite copula negative; is not/am not/are not (for...

intro_cards.title: Formal Polite Negative: ではありません
intro_cards.body:
ではありません is the formal polite negative copula — more formal than じゃありません.

• 本日は休業日ではありません。(Today is not a day off.)
• それは事実ではありません。(That is not a fact.)

You'll hear this in:
• Formal announcements and business Japanese
• Written documents
• Polite news and broadcasts

In everyday conversation, じゃありません sounds more natural.
Both are grammatically correct and polite.
